title: helianthemum pannosum subsp. frigidulum
intro: Helianthemum pannosum subsp. frigidulum, also known as rock rose, is a perennial subshrub with small, white to pink flowers and a woody base. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it beneficial for wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Helianthemum pannosum subsp. frigidulum include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of rock rose can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
